commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1277f54a52cf2b3ff63414fc851be39c03d5a81c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR splits exec outcome hashing so completed exec output remains a progress signal while failed exec hashes ignore volatile output text and keep stable failure discriminators.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +49. Total +66 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main hashes failed exec output into `resultHash`, while `getNoProgressStreak` stops accumulating when repeated calls have different hashes. I did not run a live failed-exec loop in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for the numeric `exitSignal` hash fix and real behavior proof, so this should stay in PR review rather than a ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: fix: strip volatile output from exec result hash to fix no-progress detection (#93917)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where repeated failed `exec` calls would never trigger the
no-progress circuit breaker when the error output text varied across calls.
This happens commonly with SSH timeouts, Docker connection-refused errors, and
other transient failures where timestamps or session ids in the error message
change each time while the actual failure mode is identical.

Fixes #93917

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts:213-240` | Split `hashExecToolOutcome`: `completed` keeps output in hash; `failed` strips volatile output text but preserves stable discriminators `{status, exitCode, timedOut, failureKind, exitSignal}` |
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts:639-726` | Restore `date` (completed) test to `warning`; upgrade `flaky-helper` (failed) test to `critical`/`global_circuit_breaker`; add `keeps distinct exec failure kinds` test |

## Why This Change Was Made

Split `hashExecToolOutcome` so `failed` exec results are hashed without the
volatile output text, while `completed` exec results keep output in the hash
as a real progress signal. A failed command producing different error strings
across identical retries should still count as no-progress.

## User Impact

Stuck agents that loop retrying the same failed `exec` call now correctly hit
the global circuit breaker after the configured threshold, stopping runaway
sessions instead of burning tokens forever.

## Evidence

**Real environment tested:**
- **OS**: Linux x86_64 (kernel 4.19.112)
- **Runtime**: Node.js v24.13.1
- **Commit**: `07a971b` (branch `fix/issue-93917-Bug--genericRepeat-critical-ci`)

**Exact command and full terminal output after fix:**

```
$ uname -a
Linux LIN-77DA27330FA.zte.intra 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Jun 10 09:04:49 EDT 2020 x86_64 GNU/Linux

$ node --version
v24.13.1

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/.../openclaw-worktrees/issue-93917

 ✓ |agents| tool-loop-detection.test.ts > detectToolCallLoop > blocks repeated completed exec calls despite volatile runtime details
 ✓ |agents| tool-loop-detection.test.ts > detectToolCallLoop > blocks repeated running exec calls despite volatile session details and text
 ✓ |agents| tool-loop-detection.test.ts > detectToolCallLoop > keeps changing exec output below the global no-progress breaker
 ✓ |agents| tool-loop-detection.test.ts > detectToolCallLoop > escalates repeated failed exec calls to the global breaker when status and exitCode are stable
 ...
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  59 passed (59)
   Duration  1.05s

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 10.64s
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- `keeps changing exec output below the global no-progress breaker` — completed exec with varying output stays at `warning`; varying output IS real progress ✅
- `escalates repeated failed exec calls to the global breaker when status and exitCode are stable` — failed exec with varying error text now correctly hits `critical`/`global_circuit_breaker` ✅
- `blocks repeated completed exec calls despite volatile runtime details` — completed exec with stable output still blocked at `critical` ✅
- `blocks repeated running exec calls despite volatile session details and text` — running exec with stable tail still blocked at `critical` ✅
- All 59 tests pass. No regressions in generic repeat, ping-pong, unknown-tool, message send loop, or global circuit breaker detectors.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway integration — the change is pure hash computation; coverage for non-exec tool types is unchanged.

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
